message:
test(ftw): epic completion — regression suite + DISC-FTW-001 lock-in (bd-ftw-4)

Final sub-bead of bd-ftw-epic. Adds 3 conformance regression tests
to the ftw family AND fixes a latent test-side bug:

Bug fix:
- FTW_DEPTH was defined as 4 in conformance_diff_ftw_nftw.rs but
  glibc has FTW_DEPTH=8 (4 is FTW_CHDIR). The pre-existing
  diff_nftw_depth_visits_same_set test was effectively running
  pre-order on both impls (FTW_CHDIR has no effect on visit set)
  and matched only because both impls handled the bogus flag the
  same way. Corrected to 8 — both impls now emit DP entries and
  the existing visit-set comparison still passes (DP visits added
  to both sides).

New tests:
- diff_nftw_depth_post_order_directories_after_contents: with the
  corrected FTW_DEPTH, verifies fl + lc both emit sub/d.txt before
  sub/'s DP entry (true post-order semantics)
- diff_nftw_level_field_correct: FTW info struct's `level` field
  is 0 for root, 1 for direct children, 2 for grandchildren on
  both impls
- diff_ftw_disc001_lock_in: locks in the bd-ftw2 fix — nonexistent
  dirpath returns -1 strictly on both impls (was DISC-FTW-001;
  also exercised again here through the bd-ftw-3 core port)

All 9 ftw conformance tests pass + 20 core ftw unit tests = 29
tests for the family.

Closes bd-ftw-4 and bd-ftw-epic. Family now 100% native Rust:
- core/ftw/path.rs    WalkType + WalkFlags + helpers, 200 lines + 13 tests
- core/ftw/walker.rs  FsOps trait + walk_tree driver, 290 lines + 7 tests
- abi/unistd_abi.rs   reduced from ~339 lines of recursion to ~155
                      lines of FFI adaptation (AbiStat + AbiFs +
                      ftw/nftw shims)

Improvements over previous abi-only impl:
- Walker independently unit-testable via MockFs (no real syscalls)
- Pure-safe Rust (slice-based path manipulation)
- WalkType / WalkFlags as typed wrappers instead of c_int bags
- Closure-based fs ops abstraction enables future testing flexibility
